title: ArticleLink REST API Endpoints

story.i_want_to: manage typed relationships between knowledge articles via API
story.so_that: the knowledge graph grows as agents discover connections between patterns and findings

rationale: As the knowledge wiki grows, the connections between articles become as valuable as the articles themselves. Agents discovering that a new pattern supersedes an older one, or that two conventions contradict each other, need API endpoints to record these relationships. The supersedes link type has special semantics -- creating one automatically marks the target article as superseded, keeping the knowledge base current without manual curation. The tenant validation ensures links cannot accidentally bridge tenant boundaries.

acceptance_criteria.description: POST /api/v1/article_links creates an article link. Requires role: agent or higher. Request body includes source_article_id, target_article_id, relationship_type, and optional metadata. Returns 201 with the created link.
DELETE /api/v1/article_links/:id deletes an article link. Requires role: user or higher. Returns 204 No Content on success or 404 if not found.

GET /api/v1/articles/:id/links lists all links for a given article (both outgoing and incoming). Requires role: agent or higher. Returns 200 with the list of links including the linked article's id and title.

When a link with relationship_type :supersedes is created, the target article's status is automatically set to :superseded in the same Ecto.Multi transaction. This ensures consistency -- a superseded article is always marked as such.

The create endpoint validates that both source_article_id and target_article_id refer to articles that exist within the authenticated tenant. Returns 422 if either article is not found or belongs to a different tenant.
The create endpoint validates that source_article_id != target_article_id (no self-links). Returns 422 with a descriptive error.
Controller uses `action_fallback LoopctlWeb.FallbackController` for consistent error rendering. Uses RequireRole plug for role-based access control.
Routes are added to router.ex under the authenticated API pipeline. POST and DELETE under /api/v1/article_links, GET nested under /api/v1/articles/:article_id/links.
All endpoints enforce tenant isolation. Cross-tenant link creation or access returns 404 or 422.

test_cases.name: Create article link with valid data
expected_results: Response body has link with id, source_article_id, target_article_id, relationship_type='relates_to'
Link exists in database with correct tenant_id

test_cases.name: Creating supersedes link sets target article status to superseded
expected_results: old_article status is now :superseded in the database

test_cases.name: Reject self-link via API
expected_results: Error indicates self-links are not allowed

test_cases.name: Reject link to article in different tenant
expected_results: Error indicates target article not found (article_b not visible to tenant_a)

test_cases.name: List links for article returns both outgoing and incoming
preconditions: fixture(:article_link, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, source_article_id: article_a.id, target_article_id: article_b.id, relationship_type: :relates_to})
fixture(:article_link, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, source_article_id: article_c.id, target_article_id: article_a.id, relationship_type: :derived_from})
expected_results: Returns 2 links: outgoing relates_to link to article_b and incoming derived_from link from article_c
Each link includes the linked article's id and title

test_cases.name: Delete link requires user role
preconditions: fixture(:article_link, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, source_article_id: article_a.id, target_article_id: article_b.id}) as link
expected_results: Link no longer exists in database after user deletion

test_cases.name: Tenant isolation: cannot delete another tenant's link
preconditions: fixture(:article_link, %{tenant_id: tenant_a.id, source_article_id: article_a1.id, target_article_id: article_a2.id}) as link_a
expected_results: Link still exists in database -- tenant_b cannot delete tenant_a's links

technical_notes: Controller: `defmodule LoopctlWeb.ArticleLinkController do use LoopctlWeb, :controller`. Use `action_fallback LoopctlWeb.FallbackController`.
Role enforcement: `plug LoopctlWeb.Plugs.RequireRole, :agent when action in [:create, :index]` and `plug LoopctlWeb.Plugs.RequireRole, :user when action in [:delete]`.

Routes in router.ex: `resources "/article_links", ArticleLinkController, only: [:create, :delete]` and nested `get "/articles/:article_id/links", ArticleLinkController, :index` under the authenticated pipeline.

The supersedes side-effect (setting target status to :superseded) MUST happen in the same Ecto.Multi transaction as the link creation. Pattern: `Multi.new() |> Multi.insert(:link, changeset) |> Multi.run(:supersede, fn repo, %{link: link} -> if link.relationship_type == :supersedes, do: update_target_status(repo, link), else: {:ok, nil} end)`.
Tenant validation for link creation: before inserting, verify both articles exist with matching tenant_id. This can be done in the Knowledge context's create_link/2 function (US-19.3).
The index action (GET /articles/:id/links) delegates to Knowledge.list_links_for_article/2 from US-19.3.
JSON view should serialize link fields: id, source_article_id, target_article_id, relationship_type, metadata, inserted_at. For the index action, include linked article id and title.

Testing infrastructure: (1) All tests use async: true via DataCase/ConnCase, (2) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (3) Default permissive stubs in DataCase/ConnCase setup, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex, (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests.
JSON rendering via LoopctlWeb.ArticleLinkJSON module (Phoenix 1.8 pattern).

The supersedes side-effect (target article status -> :superseded) is handled in Knowledge.create_link/2 (US-19.3), not in the controller. The controller simply calls Knowledge.create_link/2 and the domain logic handles the status transition atomically.
